name: Monasterio San Sebastián de Montehano, Santuario de la Bien Aparecida, Entrambasaguas – circular from Baranga
name_original: Monasterio San Sebastián de Montehano, Santuario de la Bien Aparecida, Entrambasaguas – circular desde Baranga
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Hazas De Cesto offer a diverse landscape in the heart of Cantabria's Trasmiera region. The municipality is characterized by its proximity to the Cantabrian Sea and inland valleys, providing varied terrain for exploration. Cyclists will find riverine scenery along the Campiazo River and rural, mountainous views throughout the area. This blend of coastal influence and inland features creates a dynamic environment for cycling. Best touring cycling routes around Hazas De Cesto The most popular touring cycling route is Monasterio San Sebastián de Montehano, Santuario de la Bien Aparecida, Entrambasaguas – circular from Baranga, a 46.1 miles (74.2 km) trail that takes 5 hours 9 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores historical sites and varied Cantabrian landscapes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Laredo Promenade – Boat across the Ría de Treto loop from Gama, a difficult 52.2 miles (83.9 km) path. This route combines coastal cycling along the Laredo Promenade with a unique boat service across the Ría de Treto. Local touring cyclists also love the View of Noja and the coast – Ajo Lighthouse loop from Gama, a 29.6 miles (47.7 km) trail leading through coastal viewpoints and towards the Ajo Lighthouse, often completed in about 3 hours 16 minutes. Touring cycling around Hazas De Cesto is defined by coastal paths, riverine scenery, and rural mountainous views. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easier Greenways to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Hazas De Cesto are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from more than 200 reviews. More than 2000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Hazas De Cesto's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Hazas de Cesto?
A: Hazas de Cesto offers a wide variety of touring cycling routes, with over 120 different options to explore. These routes cater to various skill levels, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in Hazas de Cesto?
A: The routes around Hazas de Cesto offer a good mix of challenges. You'll find 13 easy routes, 43 moderate routes, and 66 more difficult routes, providing options for both casual riders and experienced touring cyclists seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Hazas de Cesto?
A: Touring cycling in Hazas de Cesto features a diverse landscape. You can expect riverine scenery along the Campiazo River, rural and mountainous views, and coastal paths due to the region's proximity to the Cantabrian Sea. Some routes also utilize cycling-friendly 'Greenways' (Vías Verdes), which are converted railway lines ideal for relaxed rides.

Q: When is the best time of year for touring cycling in Hazas de Cesto?
A: The region's climate, being close to the Cantabrian Sea, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling. Spring and early autumn are often ideal, providing comfortable temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can also be great, especially for coastal rides, though it might be busier.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Hazas de Cesto?
A: Yes, Hazas de Cesto and the broader Cantabria region are known for their cycling-friendly 'Greenways' (Vías Verdes), which are converted railway lines with gentle gradients, making them ideal for family outings and relaxed rides. These paths often offer a safe and enjoyable experience away from traffic.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Hazas de Cesto?
A: Many touring cycling routes in the area are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. An example is the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1012371046/monasterio-san-sebastian-de-montehano-santuario-de-la-bien-aparecida-entrambasaguas-circular-from-baranga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio San Sebastián de Montehano, Santuario de la Bien Aparecida, Entrambasaguas – circular from Baranga&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical sites and varied Cantabrian landscapes.

Q: Can I find long-distance touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Absolutely. Hazas de Cesto serves as a great starting point for longer touring adventures. Routes like the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38195127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laredo Promenade – Boat across the Ría de Treto loop from Gama&lt;/a&gt; cover over 50 miles, combining coastal cycling with unique experiences like a boat crossing.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I see along touring cycling routes in Hazas de Cesto?
A: The routes offer numerous points of interest. You can cycle along the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4977738" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laredo Promenade&lt;/a&gt;, experience a unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333501" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Boat across the Ría de Treto&lt;/a&gt;, or discover natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3622793" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pozas de Langre&lt;/a&gt; tidal pools. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10508947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Noja and the coast – Ajo Lighthouse loop from Gama&lt;/a&gt; also offers stunning coastal vistas.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites accessible by touring bike?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can cycle past traditional Cantabrian architecture, visit notable churches like Santa María in Hazas de Cesto or Santiago in Praves (a historical stop on the Camino de Santiago), and explore manor houses. The Ruta del Monte Yusa also leads to the San Bernardino cave, offering a blend of cycling and exploration.

Q: Is public transport available to access touring cycling routes in Hazas de Cesto?
A: Hazas de Cesto is part of a well-connected region. While specific route access by public transport can vary, the area's infrastructure often allows for connections to nearby towns and starting points. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3991308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedreña Ferry Terminal – Pas Greenway (Astillero) loop from Gama&lt;/a&gt;, even incorporate ferry services as part of the cycling experience.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Hazas de Cesto?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, from coastal paths and riverine scenery to rural mountainous views, and the variety of routes catering to different ability levels. The unique experiences, such as the boat service across the Ría de Treto, are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes that offer a unique experience like a boat crossing?
A: Yes, a notable route that includes a unique boat crossing is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38195127" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laredo Promenade – Boat across the Ría de Treto loop from Gama&lt;/a&gt;. This route allows cyclists to utilize a boat service across the Ría de Treto, saving a considerable detour and adding an memorable element to your ride.
tips: Within this territory we find spectacular nature. We have assets of important cultural interest such as the Palacio de los Acebedo, a Herrerian Renaissance style building belonging to the 17th century, and the Palacio de los Fernández de Velasco, also from the 17th century, with elements from the 18th century, which belongs to the regional classicist. Another spectacular place is the Fuente del Francés and the Molino maquilero or aceña mill, typical of this area.

Beautiful and well-kept sanctuary located in a beautiful setting. It houses the image of La Bien Aparecida, patron saint of Cantabria. Free access. Outside is La Solana restaurant, with very good reviews.

A convent currently occupied by the Capuchin Fathers. It was declared a National Monument in 1981. Its origins go back to the Late Middle Ages. In 1597 it was the burial place of Barbara Blomberg, mother of Don Juan of Austria and lover of Emperor Charles V. It is worth making a stop to visit it. It is also a nice place to take some photos of the marsh.
